description: US-08 Block and Unblock a Customer — Manual temporary block with a mandatory free-text reason, shown verbatim at the counter. Opens with the customer detail page shell, which this and the next two batches attach their actions to.

userStories.title: Customer detail page shell at /kunden/[id]
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want a read-only page showing everything known about one customer, so later stories have a place to attach their actions. Sequencing story derived from tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.5 — it exists here so US-08, US-09 and US-10 have a host page.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /kunden/[id] renders read-only: personal data, address, household members with birthdate and current age, derived counts, group, status, current card number, certificate type and expiry, reminder count, notes
The page links to the card view at /kunden/[id]/karte
An unknown id renders a German not-found message, not a crash
The page performs no writes
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Customer status transition state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need status transitions modelled as a state machine, so illegal transitions are impossible rather than merely unlikely. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/status.ts defines Status = 'ACTIVE' | 'BLOCKED' | 'ARCHIVED' and transition(from, to)
Allowed: ACTIVE to BLOCKED, BLOCKED to ACTIVE, ACTIVE to ARCHIVED, BLOCKED to ARCHIVED
Refused with a typed IllegalStatusTransition error: any transition out of ARCHIVED, and any no-op transition
A transition to BLOCKED without a non-empty reason yields a typed BlockReasonRequired error
Every transition pair, legal and illegal, has a test
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: blockCustomer and unblockCustomer use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to pause a customer with a written reason and lift it later, so a customer can be paused without losing their number. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.2
acceptanceCriteria: blockCustomer(deps, {customerId, reason}) sets status BLOCKED and stores the trimmed reason; an empty result rejects with BlockReasonRequired
unblockCustomer(deps, {customerId}) returns the customer to ACTIVE and clears the reason
Neither use case changes the customer number, the card or any distribution record
Test asserts blocking does not free the slot — the taken-numbers query still includes it
Blocking an already-blocked customer and unblocking an active one both reject
Both write audit entries carrying the reason, with no actor field
Tested against hand-written fakes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Block fields and slot-occupancy invariant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need blocked customers to occupy their slot exactly like active ones. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.3
acceptanceCriteria: Customer.blockReason String? exists alongside status
An invariant test proves blockReason is non-null exactly when status is BLOCKED
Integration test proves the active-number unique index treats BLOCKED as occupying the slot; only ARCHIVED is exempt
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Block and unblock UI
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to block and unblock a customer and see the reason at the counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.4
acceptanceCriteria: The customer detail page offers 'Sperren' with a required multi-line reason field; save is disabled until the reason is non-empty
A blocked customer's counter screen shows the reason verbatim and in full in the verdict banner — never truncated behind a 'more' link
'Sperre aufheben' is available from the customer detail page and asks for confirmation, showing the current reason before lifting
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: blocked customer at the counter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving a block is visible and reversible. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec blocks a customer with a reason, looks them up at the counter and asserts the reason is displayed and no serve action is offered
Spec asserts an empty reason cannot be submitted
Spec lifts the block and asserts the customer is servable again with the same customer number and the same card number
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
